recipes.title: Doubles
recipes.description: Trinidad's iconic street breakfast: two soft turmeric flatbreads (bara) wrapped around curried channa with tamarind and pepper sauce.
steps: Mix flour, turmeric, cumin, yeast, sugar, salt and warm water into a sticky dough; cover and rise 1 hour.
Bloom curry powder in oil with garlic, add chickpeas, roasted geera and water to cover; simmer 25 minutes until creamy and thick.
Heat oil to 180°C and pat oiled hands to flatten balls of dough into 12 cm discs.
Fry baras one at a time, 30 seconds per side, until puffed and pale gold; drain on paper.
Place a bara on parchment, top with channa, drizzle tamarind and pepper sauce, cap with another bara and fold.

recipes.title: Roti
recipes.description: Trinidadian buss-up-shut (paratha) roti, a flaky layered flatbread torn into pieces to scoop curries.
steps: Mix flour, baking powder and salt, then add water to form a soft dough; rest 30 minutes.
Divide into 6 balls and roll each into a thin disc.
Brush with melted ghee, dust with flour, cut a slit from centre to edge and roll into a cone, then press into a ball; rest 20 minutes.
Roll each ball into a 25 cm round.
Cook on a hot tawa, brushing with ghee on both sides, for 1-2 minutes per side.
While still hot, beat the roti with a wooden spatula or clap it between hands so it 'bursses up' into flaky shreds.

recipes.title: Pelau
recipes.description: Trinidadian one-pot rice with browned chicken, pigeon peas and pumpkin, lifted by burnt-sugar caramel.
steps: Marinate chicken in green seasoning and salt for at least 1 hour.
Heat oil in a heavy pot, add brown sugar and let it caramelise to a dark amber.
Add chicken and toss to coat in the burnt sugar until deeply browned.
Add onion, garlic, pigeon peas, pumpkin and thyme; stir 2 minutes.
Stir in rice, then pour in coconut milk and stock; nestle in the whole scotch bonnet.
Cover and cook on low for 25 minutes; rest 10 minutes off heat, remove the chilli, and fluff before serving.

recipes.title: Bake and Shark
recipes.description: Trinidad beach classic from Maracas Bay: fried shark fillet in a fried bake bread topped with chadon beni and pineapple sauce.

ingredients.name: shark or firm white fish fillets

steps: Wash fish with lime juice and water, then marinate in green seasoning and salt for 30 minutes.
Mix bake flour, yeast, sugar, salt and warm water into a soft dough; rise 1 hour, then divide and roll into 15 cm discs.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry the bakes 1 minute per side until puffed and golden; drain.
Dredge fish in flour and fry 3-4 minutes per side until crisp and cooked through.
Split each bake, fill with fish, lettuce, tomato and cucumber, top generously with chadon beni and pineapple sauce.

recipes.title: Callaloo
recipes.description: Trinidadian creamy stew of dasheen leaves with okra, coconut milk and crab or salt meat.
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in butter, add pumpkin and cook 5 minutes.
Add dasheen leaves, okra, thyme, salt, scotch bonnet and coconut milk plus enough water to barely cover.
Simmer 30 minutes until vegetables are very soft.
Tuck in crabs (if using) and simmer 15 minutes more.
Remove the chilli, swizzle with a wooden swizzle stick (or blend lightly) until creamy and serve over rice.

recipes.title: Curry Channa and Aloo
recipes.description: Trinidadian curried chickpeas and potato with bloomed curry powder and ground geera, the classic roti filling.
steps: Make a curry slurry: mix curry powder with 50 ml water.
Heat oil in a heavy pot, add garlic, onion and scotch bonnet, then pour in the slurry and toast 2-3 minutes until darkened and fragrant.
Add chickpeas and potato and toss to coat.
Pour in water, add thyme and salt, and simmer 25 minutes until potatoes are tender and the gravy is thick.
Mash a few chickpeas to thicken further, finish with chadon beni and roasted geera, and serve with roti.

recipes.title: Macaroni Pie
recipes.description: Trinidadian baked macaroni custard with sharp cheddar, mustard and a slight pepper kick, served alongside Sunday lunch.
steps: Boil macaroni in salted water until just al dente, then drain.
Melt butter and toss with the hot macaroni so it doesn't stick.
Whisk eggs, evaporated milk, milk, mustard, onion, scotch bonnet, salt and pepper.
Stir in three-quarters of the cheese, then mix with the macaroni.
Pour into a greased baking dish, top with the remaining cheese.
Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until set, golden and bubbling at the edges.

recipes.title: Pholourie
recipes.description: Trinidadian Indo-Caribbean fried split-pea fritters seasoned with turmeric and cumin, served with tamarind sauce.
steps: Whisk both flours, turmeric, cumin, baking powder, yeast, salt, garlic and scotch bonnet.
Add warm water gradually to form a thick, elastic batter; rest 1 hour until bubbly.
Heat oil to 180°C.
Drop teaspoon-size balls of batter into the oil and fry 3-4 minutes until puffed, golden and hollow inside.
Drain and serve hot with tamarind sauce or mango chutney.

recipes.title: Aloo Pie
recipes.description: Trinidadian fried potato-stuffed pastry seasoned with cumin and chilli, a beloved street snack with tamarind sauce.
steps: Mix flour, baking powder, salt and water into a soft dough; rest 30 minutes.
Mash potato with garlic, geera, scotch bonnet, chadon beni and salt.
Divide dough into 8 balls; roll each into a thin oval.
Place a heap of potato filling on one half, fold and seal the edges with a fork.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry pies 3-4 minutes per side until golden and puffed.
Drain and serve hot with tamarind sauce and pepper sauce.
